ai.welcomeMessage: Hi! How can I help you today?
ai.fallbackMessage: I couldn't find an answer to that. Would you like to contact support?

fields.name: invite_members
fields.description: Open the invite members dialog to send team invitations. Use when user wants to invite someone, add a team member, or send an invite. If the user mentions specific emails or a role, pass them to pre-fill the dialog.

role.description: Role for the invitees (defaults to member)

emails.description: Email addresses to pre-fill in the invite dialog

fields.name: list_api_keys
fields.description: Get the list of API keys (sync secrets) for the current project. Returns key names, creation dates, and last used dates. Call this when you need to reference keys by name before other operations.

fields.name: manage_api_keys
fields.description: Open the API key manager dialog. Lists all existing keys with the ability to create new keys or revoke existing ones. Use when user wants to view, list, manage, or delete API keys.

fields.name: generate_api_key
fields.description: Generate a new API key (sync secret) for the current project. The raw key is securely delivered via a Reveal button — it never appears in conversation history. Use when user asks to create, generate, or add an API key. Always provide a name — do NOT ask the user for one. If they specified a name, use it. Otherwise, pick a sensible default like 'default', 'development', or 'api-key-1'.
examples: add an API key called production

name.description: Label for the key (e.g. production, staging, ci). Lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens only. Always provide a value — use the user's choice if given, otherwise default to 'default'.

fields.name: add_allowed_domain
fields.description: Add a domain to the allowed domains list for embed security. Use when user wants to whitelist a domain, allow a new domain, add localhost for testing, or configure CORS/embed domains. This saves immediately.
examples: add my domain to the embed allowlist

fields.name: list_tools
fields.description: Get the list of defined tools for this product. Returns tool names, descriptions, and types. Call this when user asks what you can do or what tools are available.

fields.name: create_new_tool
fields.description: Navigate to create a new tool that the AI can suggest to users. Use when user wants to add a new tool, create an automation, or define a new task for the AI assistant.

fields.name: open_tools
fields.description: Navigate to the tools page to view and manage AI-suggested tools. tools are buttons the AI can suggest to users. Use when user asks about tools, tasks, automation, or configuring what the AI can do.

fields.name: escalate
fields.description: Connect user with human support when they request help or have account issues. Use when user explicitly asks to talk to a person, contact support, or has issues that require human assistance like billing problems or account access.
examples: I need help from a human

fields.name: get_product_settings
fields.description: Get the current product configuration. Returns brand name, features enabled, and AI settings. Call this when user asks about settings or configuration.

fields.name: get_conversation_stats
fields.description: Get AI conversation statistics for the past 30 days. Returns total conversations, resolution rate, feedback, and top questions. Call this when user asks about usage, performance, or analytics.

fields.name: list_snippets
fields.description: Get the list of custom instruction snippets. Returns snippet titles and excerpts. Call this when user asks about custom instructions or AI behavior customization.

fields.name: get_help_center_status
fields.description: Get the overall help center setup status. Returns what's configured and what's missing. Call this when helping with initial setup or onboarding.

fields.name: list_team_members
fields.description: Get the list of team members and pending invitations. Returns member emails, roles, and status. Call this before suggesting invite or role-change tools.

fields.name: get_source_sync_status
fields.description: Get detailed sync status for a knowledge source. Returns last sync time, document count, errors, and progress. Call this when user asks about sync status or content freshness.

source_id.description: The source ID to check (from list_sources)

fields.name: list_sources
fields.description: Get the list of configured knowledge sources. Returns source IDs, names, types, and sync status. Call this before suggesting source-specific tools to know what exists. Example: Before suggesting 'crawl website', check if a website source exists.

fields.name: set_usage_alert
fields.description: Configure spending alerts to get notified when monthly usage exceeds a threshold. Use when user wants to set up budget alerts, spending notifications, cost limits, or be notified when they're about to hit their usage cap.
examples: alert me when I spend over $500 on Slack
warn me before I hit my limit

channel.description: Where to send notifications (email or slack)

threshold.description: Dollar amount threshold for the alert

fields.name: open_billing
fields.description: Navigate to the Billing page to view subscription plan, usage stats, invoices, and payment settings. Use when user asks about billing, invoices, subscription, plan details, or payment information.

fields.name: update_user_permissions
fields.description: Update a user's permission level for a specific RESOURCE (production, staging, analytics, billing, or team). Use when user mentions a specific resource like 'production' or 'staging' along with a permission level (admin/edit/view/none). This controls what they can do in that specific area, not their team role.
examples: Give Sarah admin access to production
Give John admin access to staging
Give Emily admin access to analytics
Give Sarah view access to production
Set Michael to edit on team settings

resource.description: The resource area to update permissions for

userName.description: Name or email of the user to update

fields.name: demote_to_member
fields.description: Change an organization admin back to regular member role. This changes their TEAM ROLE, not resource permissions. Use only when user explicitly says 'demote' or 'remove admin role' without mentioning a specific resource.

fields.name: promote_to_admin
fields.description: Promote a team member to organization admin role, giving them ability to invite/remove team members. This changes their TEAM ROLE, not resource permissions. Use only when user explicitly says 'make admin' or 'promote to admin' without mentioning a specific resource.

fields.name: remove_team_member
fields.description: Remove a user from the organization and revoke their access. Use when user wants to remove someone from the team, revoke access, delete a team member, or kick someone out. Warning: this is irreversible.

fields.name: cancel_invitation
fields.description: Cancel a pending invitation so the link no longer works. Use when user wants to revoke access before someone accepts, cancel an invite, remove a pending invitation, or rescind an invite.

fields.name: resend_invitation
fields.description: Resend a pending invitation email to a user who hasn't accepted yet. Use when user asks to resend an invite, remind someone about their invitation, send another invite email, or nudge a pending invitee.

fields.name: view_pending_invitations
fields.description: View all pending team invitations that haven't been accepted yet. Use when user asks about outstanding invites, who hasn't joined yet, wants to check invitation status, or see pending team invites.

fields.name: open_team_settings
fields.description: Navigate to the Team settings page to view and manage team members, invitations, and permissions. Use when user asks about team, members, users, invites, or wants to manage who has access to the organization.

fields.name: search_conversations
fields.description: Search and filter individual chat conversations by keyword or topic. Use when user wants to find specific conversations, look up what users asked about, see chats mentioning a topic, or browse conversation history.
examples: find chats where users asked about pricing

query.description: Search query to find matching conversations

fields.name: export_conversations_csv
fields.description: Export conversations to a CSV file for the specified date range. Use when user wants to download conversation data, export chat logs, or get analytics data for external analysis.

fields.name: view_analytics_last_90_days
fields.description: View analytics dashboard for the last 90 days (quarterly). Use when user asks about quarterly performance or longer-term trends.

fields.name: view_analytics_last_30_days
fields.description: View analytics dashboard for the last 30 days. Use when user asks about this month's performance or monthly metrics.

fields.name: view_analytics_last_7_days
fields.description: View analytics dashboard for the last 7 days. Use when user asks about this week's performance, recent metrics, or short-term analytics.

fields.name: view_ai_analytics
fields.description: View AI assistant performance metrics including resolution rate, escalation rate, and message volume. Use when user asks about AI analytics, chatbot stats, how the AI is performing, or assistant metrics.

fields.name: view_search_analytics
fields.description: View search analytics including popular queries, failed searches, and search trends. Use when user asks about search analytics, popular searches, what people are searching for, or search performance.

fields.name: delete_source
fields.description: Delete a knowledge source and remove all its content from the knowledge base. This is a destructive tool that cannot be undone. Use when user wants to remove a source, disconnect an integration, or delete imported content.

source_id.description: ID of the source to delete (from list_sources)

fields.name: resync_source
fields.description: Trigger a re-sync for a knowledge source to refresh content. Use when user wants to update content, refresh docs, re-crawl a website, or sync the latest changes from a source. Call list_sources first if source_id is unknown.

source_id.description: ID of the source to resync (from list_sources)

fields.name: connect_cloud_storage
fields.description: Connect a cloud storage bucket (AWS S3 or Google Cloud Storage) to sync documents. Use when user wants to connect S3, GCS, cloud storage, or sync files from buckets.

fields.name: crawl_website
fields.description: Crawl a website by URL to import content. Works for help centers, documentation sites, marketing sites, and any public website. Use when user wants to import content from a website, help center, docs site, or marketing site. Examples: Zendesk Guide sites, Intercom help centers, GitBook sites, custom documentation sites, company marketing pages.

fields.name: add_new_source
fields.description: Navigate to add a new knowledge source. Opens a wizard to connect external documentation, help centers, knowledge bases, websites, or cloud storage buckets. Use when user wants to import content, connect a source, add an integration, or sync from an external platform. Extract the source type and URL if mentioned in the user's message.
examples: can you help me setup my help center import
how do I import my help center

url.description: URL of the source to connect (for website crawl)

name.description: Display name for the source if mentioned

type.description: Source type ID if user mentions a specific type. Values: 'website' for crawling any website (help centers, docs sites, marketing sites), 'bucket' for cloud storage buckets (S3/GCS)

fields.name: update_fallback_message
fields.description: Update the fallback message shown when the AI cannot answer a question. Use when user wants to customize what happens when the AI doesn't know, or change the escalation message. This saves immediately.
examples: set fallback message to Let me connect you with support
change what the AI says when it doesn't know
update the I don't know response

message.description: The new fallback message to display

fields.name: update_primary_color
fields.description: Update the primary/accent color used throughout the help center. Use when user wants to change their brand color, theme color, accent color, or primary color. Convert any color name the user mentions to a hex code before calling. This saves immediately.
examples: update the accent color to blue

hex.description: Hex color code including the # prefix (e.g. #22c55e for green, #3b82f6 for blue). Always convert color names to hex before calling.

fields.name: set_suggested_questions
fields.description: Set the suggested questions that appear in the AI chat widget. Use when user wants to configure starter questions, conversation starters, or example queries. This saves immediately.
examples: set starter questions to How do I get started, What are the pricing plans

fields.name: update_ai_welcome_message
fields.description: Update the welcome message shown when users open the AI chat. Use when user wants to change the greeting, update the intro message, or customize how the AI introduces itself. This saves immediately.
examples: set welcome message to Hi! How can I help?
change the greeting to Welcome to our help center

message.description: The new welcome message to display

fields.name: update_ai_assistant_name
fields.description: Update the AI assistant's display name shown in the chat widget. Use when user wants to rename the chatbot, change the assistant name, or personalize the AI identity. This saves immediately.
examples: change assistant name to Support Bot

name.description: The new name for the AI assistant

fields.name: update_brand_name
fields.description: Update the brand name displayed in the help center. Use when user wants to change their company name, brand name, or help center title. This saves immediately.
examples: change my brand name to Acme
update the title to My Help Center

name.description: The new brand name to display

fields.name: configure_suggested_questions
fields.description: Configure the suggested questions that appear in the AI chat widget. Use when user wants to set up suggested questions, conversation starters, or example questions for the AI.

fields.name: add_footer_link
fields.description: Add a new link to the help center footer. Use when user wants to add a footer link, add bottom navigation, or include additional links in footer.

fields.name: update_logo
fields.description: Open the logo uploader to change or update the help center logo. Use when user wants to upload a new logo, change the logo, or update branding image.

fields.name: change_theme
fields.description: Open the theme selector to switch to a different visual theme preset. Use when user wants to change theme, switch themes, or try a different look.

fields.name: toggle_dark_mode
fields.description: Toggle between dark and light mode. Use when user asks to 'toggle dark mode', 'switch theme', 'change to dark/light', or generally wants to flip their current theme without specifying which one they want.

fields.name: disable_dark_mode
fields.description: Switch to light mode / disable dark theme. Use when user explicitly wants to turn OFF dark mode, disable dark mode, or switch TO light theme/mode.

fields.name: enable_dark_mode
fields.description: Switch to dark mode / dark theme. Use when user explicitly wants to turn ON dark mode, enable dark mode, or switch TO dark theme.

fields.name: disable_ai_assistant
fields.description: Disable the AI chat assistant widget to hide it from users. Use when user wants to turn off, disable, or deactivate the AI assistant or chatbot.

fields.name: enable_ai_assistant
fields.description: Enable the AI chat assistant widget so users can ask questions and get AI-powered answers. Use when user wants to turn on, enable, or activate the AI assistant or chatbot.

fields.name: open_configure
fields.description: Navigate to the Configure page to set up and customize the AI assistant behavior, prompts, and response settings. Use when user asks about configuring the assistant, AI setup, or customizing how the AI responds.

fields.name: open_analytics
fields.description: Navigate to the Analytics dashboard to view help center performance metrics, search analytics, and usage statistics. Use when user asks about analytics, metrics, statistics, performance, or reports.

fields.name: open_settings
fields.description: Navigate to the Configure page to manage help center appearance, branding, AI assistant, and other options. Use when user asks about settings, configuration, customization, or preferences.

fields.name: open_knowledge
fields.description: Navigate to the Knowledge page to view and manage all connected knowledge sources. Use when user asks about knowledge sources, integrations, connected documentation, syncing content, or importing articles from external platforms.
